title: options.Quiet should be passed into preprocessor
body:
Preprocessor should not be printing warnings in quiet mode.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago This will allow us to test against podman since quiet means quiet.

After a day's thought, I feel more strongly that this should not merge. Someone passing `-q` is, IMO, saying "Shut up and give me the image ID, but hey, if something goes wrong, I'd like to know about it". This PR does not honor that. But I'm just one small voice.
Would have to check if Warnings are currently silenced by -q? @nalind WDYT?  Should Warnings be exposed when running in --quiet mode?
`--quiet` controls progress messages ("STEP ...", "COMMIT", pull progress, etc.), and hasn't historically affected logging level.  Is there an issue that's motivating this change?
There is a bug in podman-remote that looks like it is effected by this. but I will see why it is happening there.
